# claude-auto-retry

> CLI that auto-retries Claude Code sessions. When a subscription usage/session
> limit hits ("You've hit your session limit · resets 2am"), it waits until the
> printed reset time and sends "continue" automatically. Sustained API overload
> (`API Error: 529 … overloaded_error`, 500/502/503/504) gets exponential backoff
> with jitter; safeguard/AUP false positives ("safeguards flagged this message")
> get a bounded immediate re-send. Claude runs inside a PTY the tool owns (node-pty
> + a headless terminal emulator) — no tmux required; run it inside your own
> tmux/screen if you want the session to survive disconnects.

Install: `npm i -g claude-auto-retry && claude-auto-retry install` — then use `claude`
as usual.

Key facts:

- Detects the real Claude Code renders verbatim: `5-hour limit reached - resets 3pm`,
  `You've hit your session limit · resets 2am (Europe/Zurich)`, `You've hit your weekly
  limit · resets May 28 at 7pm`, `Claude usage limit reached`, `You're out of extra
  usage`, `API Error: 529 {"type":"overloaded_error"…}`, `API Error: Server is
  temporarily limiting requests (not your usage limit)`, `<model>'s safeguards flagged
  this message`.
- Dismisses the `/rate-limit-options` menu with Escape before submitting the retry —
  the highlighted option varies by version, so it never confirms "Upgrade your plan".
- Safe by design: the retry is written straight into the PTY hosting claude (it can
  never leak into a shell or another app), it stops the moment the user (or Claude)
  resumes, and error detection is anchored to the live screen tail so quoted errors
  in scrollback can't trigger a retry.
- Timezone- and DST-aware reset-time parsing (incl. dated weekly resets); `--print`
  mode supported; optional config at `~/.claude-auto-retry.json` with safe fallbacks
  for invalid values.
- TypeScript run via Node type stripping (no build step); Node >= 23.6; MIT license;
  220+ tests.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/chris-karl/claude-auto-retry/blob/master/README.md): features, configuration, every error render detected
- [DESIGN-NOTES](https://github.com/chris-karl/claude-auto-retry/blob/master/DESIGN-NOTES.md): architecture — StopFailure hook event channel vs. terminal scraper, overload backoff design
- [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/chris-karl/claude-auto-retry/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md): release history

## Install for a user (agent-friendly, non-interactive)

- `npm install -g claude-auto-retry`
- `claude-auto-retry install` — shell wrapper; takes effect in new shells
- `claude-auto-retry install-hook` — recommended: event-driven overload detection via Claude Code's StopFailure hook
- Verify: `claude-auto-retry status`
- Remove: `claude-auto-retry uninstall` and `claude-auto-retry uninstall-hook`
